titleStmt.title: Letter from Patrick O'Brien to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, 21 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Patrick O'Brien

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Patrick O'Brien to the lord lieutenant concerning O'Brien's distressed circumstances due to his dependence on a small amount of outdoor relief as a result of unemployment.In 1916 the lord lieutenant of Ireland was Ivor Churchill Guest, 1st Viscount Wimborne (1873-1939). Guest worked closely alongside Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) in the administration of Ireland. Following the suppression of the Rising, Guest, under pressure, was forced to resign as Lord Lieutenant. A later inquiry into the Rising exonerated Guest and held Augustine Birrell (1850-1933) chiefly responsible for the events that occurred. Guest would continue to serve as Lord Lieutenant until 1918.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Glenbane June 21st, 16 Dear Lord Lieutenant May it be pleasing to your good
excellency to accept these lines
from a poor distressed man. I
am out of my health for the last
five years and cannot work. I
have only three shillings a week
to support me, that is given to
me as out door relief, and I
want to put my claim before
you, confidently expecting that
I will be successful.
you will not ever regard or be
the worst of the realm, if you
take compassion on me, and keep
me from a lingering starvation I remain your obedient P O'Brien Address)Patrick O'Brien. Glenbane, Lattin. Co Tipperary I implore your excellency's donation.
On His Majesty's Service.
THE REGISTRAR,
Dublin Castle.
